description:
You are on a CI-runner that just produced three build artifacts for the “web-api” service.  
    Inspect the directory  
        /home/user/cicd/builds  
    and you will find these exact files (no others):  

        artifact-1.2.3.jar  
        artifact-1.2.4.jar  
        artifact-1.3.0.jar  

    Task – do three things in any way you like:

    1. Create a new directory called  
           /home/user/cicd/releases  

    2. Copy only the file that represents the newest version (artifact-1.3.0.jar) into that new directory and rename it to:  
           prod-artifact.jar  
       • Do not remove or rename any of the original files inside /home/user/cicd/builds.  

    3. Produce a very small log so the pipeline can audit what you did.  
       • The log must be created at exactly:  
             /home/user/cicd/pipeline.log  
       • The log must contain exactly two non-blank lines, in this precise order and with no extra whitespace, newlines or quotes:  

             COPY_SRC=/home/user/cicd/builds/artifact-1.3.0.jar  
             COPY_DEST=/home/user/cicd/releases/prod-artifact.jar  

       • No header lines, comments, trailing spaces or additional blank lines are allowed – the automated test will fail if anything differs (even a single character).  

    When you finish, the tester will verify:  
        • /home/user/cicd/releases exists.  
        • /home/user/cicd/releases/prod-artifact.jar exists and is an exact byte-for-byte copy of /home/user/cicd/builds/artifact-1.3.0.jar.  
        • /home/user/cicd/pipeline.log exists and its two lines are exactly as specified above.  
        • All three original JAR files are still present in /home/user/cicd/builds.  

    You can use any shell commands you prefer, just achieve the described end-state.
truth:
Initial filesystem (before the agent starts):

    Directories:
        /home/user/cicd
        /home/user/cicd/builds

    Files and contents (all three JARs have the same placeholder payload so a byte-wise comparison is possible):

        /home/user/cicd/builds/artifact-1.2.3.jar
            --> ASCII text: "dummy jar payload v1.2.3\n"
        /home/user/cicd/builds/artifact-1.2.4.jar
            --> ASCII text: "dummy jar payload v1.2.4\n"
        /home/user/cicd/builds/artifact-1.3.0.jar
            --> ASCII text: "dummy jar payload v1.3.0\n"

    Expected state after correct completion:

    Directories:
        /home/user/cicd/releases      (must exist; any permission ≥ 755)
    
    Files:
        1. /home/user/cicd/releases/prod-artifact.jar
           • Exact byte-for-byte duplicate of /home/user/cicd/builds/artifact-1.3.0.jar

        2. /home/user/cicd/pipeline.log
           • Size: 111 bytes (including the single newline after the first line)
           • Exact content (encoded in UTF-8):

COPY_SRC=/home/user/cicd/builds/artifact-1.3.0.jar
COPY_DEST=/home/user/cicd/releases/prod-artifact.jar

           • Only one newline character separates the two lines; no terminating newline is required after the second line, but its presence is tolerated.

    None of the original files inside /home/user/cicd/builds may be modified, removed or renamed.
